title: [InstCombine] Canonicalize `sub mask, X -> ~X` when high bits are ignored

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i64 @test_trunc_demand(i64 %x) {
  %sub = sub i64 65535, %x
  %trunc = trunc i64 %sub to i16
  %ext = zext i16 %trunc to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i64 @test_trunc_demand(i64 %x) {
  %not = xor i64 %x, -1
  %trunc = trunc i64 %not to i16
  %ext = zext i16 %trunc to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Uses truncation to create demanded bits constraint instead of the shift pattern shown in the summary. Shows the optimization works with different downstream operations that constrain demanded bits.

alive2.src:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i64 @test_trunc_demand(i64 %x) {
  %sub = sub i64 65535, %x
  %trunc = trunc i64 %sub to i16
  %ext = zext i16 %trunc to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

; Function Attrs: mustprogress norecurse nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none)
define range(i64 0, 65536) i64 @test_trunc_demand(i64 %x) local_unnamed_addr #0 {
  %trunc = and i64 %x, 65535
  %ext = xor i64 %trunc, 65535
  ret i64 %ext
}

attributes #0 = { mustprogress norecurse nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none) }
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i64 @test_trunc_demand(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %sub = sub i64 65535, %x
  %trunc = trunc i64 %sub to i16
  %ext = zext i16 %trunc to i64
  ret i64 %ext
}
=>
define i64 @test_trunc_demand(i64 %x) nofree willreturn memory(none) {
#0:
  %trunc = and i64 %x, 65535
  %ext = xor i64 %trunc, 65535
  %#range_0_%ext = !range i64 %ext, i64 0, i64 65536
  ret i64 %#range_0_%ext
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i32 @i32_small_mask_0xFF(i32 %x) {
  %sub = sub i32 255, %x
  %trunc = trunc i32 %sub to i8
  %ext = zext i8 %trunc to i32
  ret i32 %ext
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define i32 @i32_small_mask_0xFF(i32 %x) {
  %not = xor i32 %x, -1
  %trunc = trunc i32 %not to i8
  %ext = zext i8 %trunc to i32
  ret i32 %ext
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Uses i32 type with 0xFF (8-bit) mask instead of i64 with 0xFFFFF (20-bit) mask mentioned in summary. Tests smaller low-bit mask on different integer width.
